items.name: Iran-backed actors (MOIS-linked)
items.targets: Stryker (MedTech), U.S. critical infrastructure, Iranian domestic internet infrastructure (blackout operations), Qatar entities amid Middle East conflict
items.ttps: T1485 Data Destruction (wiper attacks), T1566 Phishing, T1598 Phishing for Information, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware connections with Qilin), T1499 DDoS, T1589 Gather Victim Identity Information (fake shelter danger calls for psychological operations)

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korean APT)
items.targets: AllSecure CEO, developers via fake job opportunities and LinkedIn social engineering
items.ttps: T1566.001 Spearphishing Attachment, T1566.003 Spearphishing via Service (LinkedIn), T1204.002 User Execution: Malicious File, T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Hundreds of Salesforce Experience Cloud customers in mass-scanning campaign using modified AuraInspector tool
items.ttps: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1530 Data from Cloud Storage Object, T1213 Data from Information Repositories, T1087 Account Discovery

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian infrastructure remains on alert due to global phishing campaigns and increased threat actor activity. Meta disabled 150K accounts linked to Southeast Asia scam centers affecting Canada among other regions. A phishing campaign abusing .arpa domains specifically mentioned Canadian targeting. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported in the 48-hour period.
canada_landscape.retailers: None identified. No specific incidents reported affecting Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's, or other major Canadian retail chains.
canada_landscape.financial: None identified. No specific incidents reported affecting CIBC, RBC, TD Bank, Scotiabank, BMO, or other Canadian financial institutions.
